_doc: Food Inflammation Index (FII) parameter table — the single source of which per-100g signals enter the per-food inflammatory-potential score, each one's direction and weight. Adapted from the Food Inflammation Index idea (Wang 2024) to the composition columns this archive holds; directions are taken from open biomarker literature, NOT the licensed DII per-parameter effect scores. `dir` is +1 pro-inflammatory / -1 anti-inflammatory; `weight` scales a parameter's contribution. Read by src/core/fii.ts and scripts/build-inflammation-reference.mjs. Two parameters are DERIVED, not read straight off the vector: `fatQuality_g` = (monoFat+polyFat) − (satFat+transFat) so fat is scored once by quality instead of letting a food's saturated and unsaturated fractions cancel under independent z-scores; `freeSugar_g` = max(0, sugar − 2·fibre) estimates the free-sugar fraction from the 1:2 fibre:free-sugar dual carbohydrate-quality ratio, so a whole food's intrinsic, matrix-bound sugar is not penalised the way a refined sugar is.

parameters.cite: fat quality (MUFA+PUFA − SFA−trans), derived from the fat fractions: Wang 2024 FII

polyphenols (key anti driver): Cassidy 2015; Dryer-Beers 2024; Wang 2024 FII. One-sided: referent centre pinned at 0 (no measurable polyphenols = baseline, so a low-polyphenol food never reads pro) and scale = the median content of the foods that carry a Phenol-Explorer value (the typical polyphenol-bearing food ≈ 1 unit anti); fixed 100 mg/100g scale only while coverage is <50 foods (Pérez-Jiménez 2010)

free-sugar estimate = max(0, sugar − 2·fibre), the 1:2 fibre:free-sugar dual carbohydrate-quality ratio (Liu 2020; Drewnowski 2022); intrinsic matrix-bound sugar is not penalised (Della Corte 2025; Reyneke 2025)
